title: 10 best herbal capsules for menstrual cramps
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for menstrual cramps are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with menstrual cramps. These capsules are formulated with a blend of herbs such as Cimicifuga racemosa, Paeonia lactiflora, Zingiber officinale, Angelica sinensis, Vitex agnus-castus, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Lavandula angustifolia, Trifolium pratense, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, menstrual cramps can negatively impact daily lives, causing missed work or school, social isolation, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a natural compound called valerenic acid, which has been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the capsules' high concentration of bioflavonoids helps to improve blood flow and reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause cramping. As a result, guelder rose capsules provide effective relief from menstrual cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms naturally and maintain a healthy quality of life during their period.
remedy_properties: Spasmolytic effects: The capsules contain compounds that relax uterine muscles, reducing contractions and pain associated with menstrual cramps.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: Guelder Rose contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to menstrual cramp relief.
Pain-relieving properties: The capsules contain salicin, a natural analgesic similar to aspirin, which helps alleviate pain associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Aesculetin: Aesculetin, a coumarin derivative, has been reported to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Viburnin: Viburnin, a flavonoid glycoside, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which could contribute to the relief of menstrual cramp symptoms.
Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpenoid saponin, has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may help reduce menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Viburnum opulus, also known as guelder rose berries, are used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to their reported analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark of Viburnum opulus is used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Viburnum opulus are used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to their reported analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried viburnum opulus flowers from a trusted source.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 1 gram of beeswax and 5 grams of glycerin.
Fill 250 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling tool.
Label and date the capsules for future reference and safe storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce uterine contractions and ease pain. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramp severity and frequency. Additionally, ginger has been shown to increase the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that help to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual discomfort. As a result, taking herbal ginger capsules may provide significant relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation in the body, relieving menstrual cramp discomfort.
Pain-relieving properties: Ginger has been shown to have analgesic and antipyretic effects, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Digestive benefits: Herbal ginger capsules can also help to soothe digestive issues often experienced during menstruation, such as bloating, nausea, and diarrhea, further reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols and Shogaols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.

Gingerol-6 (Gingerdiol): It is a potent anti-inflammatory compound that inhibits the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation during menstrual cramps.

Zingiberone: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps, and also has a sedative effect that can help with relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The underground stem of a plant that stores nutrients, used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to its active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Roots: Roots: The underground part of a plant that anchors it, used to make capsules for menstrual cramps because of its high concentration of gingerols and shogaols that help alleviate cramps and other symptoms.

Buds: Buds: The early stage of a plant's flower or leaf, used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to its higher concentration of active compounds compared to mature parts of the plant, providing faster relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes from a reliable source and clean them thoroughly.
Crush the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 250g of the powder with 100g of vegetable cellulose powder as a capsule filler.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and attach it to a machine that can make 250 capsules in 10 minutes.
Store the herbal zingiber officinale capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with menstrual cramps because of its unique compound, curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Curcumin works by reducing prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to severe menstrual cramps. Additionally, turmeric's ability to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation helps alleviate cramping symptoms, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with menstruation. Regular consumption of turmeric capsules can significantly reduce menstrual cramp severity and frequency, making it a natural and effective solution for women seeking relief.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which help reduce menstrual cramp severity by blocking prostaglandins, hormones that cause uterine contractions.

Cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibition: Curcumin in turmeric capsules inhibits the production of COX-2, an enzyme that contributes to inflammation and pain during menstruation, thereby reducing menstrual cramp symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and menstrual cramp severity. By scavenging these free radicals, turmeric capsules may help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a demethoxy derivative of curcumin, contributes to the anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties of Curcuma longa, helping to reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Tumerone (also known as Curcumenol): Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in the reduction of menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are widely used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate menstrual cramps.
Roots: Turmeric roots are commonly used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramp pain and promote overall women's health.
Buds: Curcuma longa buds are another popular ingredient due to their high curcumin content and potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa rhizomes when the tops begin to yellow and dry out naturally taking about 8-10 months.
Clean and dry the rhizomes completely to ensure they are free from dirt and moisture taking 1-2 days.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at a temperature below 40°c.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill the powder mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them immediately to preserve potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with menstrual cramps because of its natural relaxant properties that soothe the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and pain. The apigenin present in chamomile has a calming effect on the body, helping to ease anxiety and promote relaxation, which can alleviate stress-related menstrual cramps. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation in the pelvis, further minimizing discomfort and cramping associated with menstruation. As a result, chamomile capsules provide effective natural relief from menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to menstrual cramps.
Antioxidants: Chamomile's antioxidants help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to menstrual cramp severity and duration.
Muscle relaxants: Chamomile's flavonoids, such as apigenin, have a calming effect on smooth muscle tissue, which helps to relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid apigenin found in Matricaria chamomilla capsules helps with menstrual cramps by acting as a muscle relaxant and reducing inflammation in the uterus.

Luteolin: A flavonoid luteolin present in Matricaria chamomilla capsules helps alleviate menstrual cramps by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances responsible for pain and inflammation.

Alpha-bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alpha-bisabolol in Matricaria chamomilla capsules helps to reduce menstrual cramps by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, thereby reducing the severity of cramps and associated discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for menstrual cramp relief due to their high concentration of apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Seeds: Utilized for their ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by regulating hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: Employed for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe menstrual cramp discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when the dew is still present on the petals.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 1 minute.
Mix 250mg of the ground powder with 125mg of vegetable cellulose in a small bowl for capsule filling.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and seal the capsules at a rate of 50 capsules per minute.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain menthol, a natural compound that relaxes muscles and reduces inflammation. When taken orally, peppermint oil can help ease spasms and contractions in the uterus, providing relief from the intense pain associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the digestive system can also help alleviate bloating and discomfort commonly experienced during menstruation, allowing women to feel more comfortable and relaxed throughout their periods.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint capsules contain menthol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to relax muscles and reduce pain associated with menstrual cramps.

Antispasmodic effects: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties help to ease uterine contractions and reduce the intensity of menstrual cramps by relaxing the smooth muscle in the uterus and reducing inflammation.
Digestive benefits: Peppermint capsules can also help to alleviate symptoms of PMS-related bloating, gas, and digestive discomfort, which often accompany menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol, a terpene found in peppermint, helps alleviate menstrual cramps by relaxing the smooth muscles in the uterus and reducing inflammation.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound found in peppermint, exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to ease menstrual cramp pain and reduce prostaglandin levels.

Beta-caryophyllene: Beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene found in peppermint, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce menstrual cramp pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Stems: They contain menthol and other compounds that help relax muscles, reduce pain, and ease menstrual cramp symptoms.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from a pesticide-free area.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40°c for 2 days.
Grind 100g of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powder with 90g of vegetable cellulose and 10g of magnesium stearate.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain isoflavones, a type of phytoestrogen that mimics the effects of estrogen in the body. These isoflavones help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms. Additionally, red clover's natural anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and contribute to menstrual cramps. By taking herbal red clover capsules, women may experience significant relief from menstrual cramp discomfort and improved overall well-being during their periods.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover capsules contain a type of flavonoid called isoflavones, which have been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce prostaglandins, helping to alleviate menstrual cramp pain.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens in red clover capsules can bind to estrogen receptors in the body, reducing the production of prostaglandins and other hormones that contribute to menstrual cramping.
Vasodilatory effects: Red clover's vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, which can increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the uterus, leading to relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones (specifically, Genistein and Formononetin): These compounds have estrogenic properties that help balance hormonal fluctuations, which can contribute to menstrual cramps.
Coumarins (specifically, Trifolin and Trifosin): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which may help alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort.

Phenolic acids (specifically, Chlorogenic acid and Caffeic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to their isoflavone content, which may help alleviate symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used due to their isoflavone and coumarin content, which may help relax the uterine muscles and reduce pain.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their isoflavone content, specifically genistein and formononetin, which may help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by reducing prostaglandins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest trifolium pratense flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and drying time of 2-3 weeks.
Dry the flowers in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a natural binding agent such as gum arabic and 1 part of vegetable cellulose.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and fill each capsule with 250 milligrams of the herbal material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a natural blend of herbs that work to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with menstruation. The active ingredients in dong quai, such as ferulic acid and coumarins, have been shown to relax the uterine muscles, reducing contractions and spasms that cause cramping. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of dong quai help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, further contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Dong quai capsules have an antispasmodic effect, which helps to relax and ease muscle contractions in the uterus, reducing menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of dong quai help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterine lining, which can contribute to menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.

Vasodilatory property: Dong quai's vasodilatory effect helps to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the uterus and surrounding areas, which can help alleviate menstrual cramp pain by reducing constriction of blood vessels.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce menstrual pain and discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals that contribute to cramping.
Ligustilide: A sesquiterpene lactone compound, ligustilide has a sedative effect on the uterus, helping to relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms that cause menstrual cramps.

Butylphthalide: A sesquiterpene compound, butylphthalide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce menstrual pain and discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and prostaglandins that contribute to cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Stems: Contain various bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Leaves: High in ferulic acid and other antioxidants, which help reduce prostaglandins that cause menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried angelica sinensis root and clean it thoroughly to remove impurities.
Grind the dried angelica sinensis root into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Mix the powder with 20-30 grams of cellulose powder or other capsule filler to achieve a consistent texture.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and seal the capsules at a speed of 50-100 capsules per minute.
Label and store the herbal angelica sinensis capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain valerenic acid, a compound that has natural relaxant properties. This property allows it to calm the uterine muscles, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps. Additionally, valerian root is known for its ability to increase blood flow, which can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. As a result, women who take herbal valerian capsules may experience significant relief from menstrual cramp symptoms, allowing them to manage their monthly cycle with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian root's sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and stress associated with menstrual cramps, while its anti-inflammatory effects can alleviate pain and discomfort.
Muscle relaxation: Valerian's ability to relax muscles can help ease uterine contractions, reducing the severity of menstrual cramps and making them more manageable.

GABA-ergic activity: Valerian root contains GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation. By increasing GABA levels, valerian capsules can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can help alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid esters: They help with menstrual cramps by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, thereby alleviating muscle spasms and pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Valerenic acid: It helps with menstrual cramps by acting on the nervous system to reduce inflammation and modulate pain pathways, leading to a decrease in menstrual cramp severity.
Valeranone: It contributes to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects of Valeriana officinalis, helping to reduce menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which have a sedative effect and help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Seeds: The seeds are sometimes used in combination with roots or rhizomes to enhance the sedative effect and provide additional relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in its dormant stage.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin and a small amount of water to form a thick paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared paste using a capsule filler machine or a spoon and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine muscle contractions and lead to menstrual cramp discomfort. By inhibiting the production of these prostaglandins, feverfew capsules alleviate cramping symptoms, providing relief from pelvic pain, bloating, and mood swings associated with menstruation. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to manage menstrual cramps without relying on medication or surgery.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide content: Feverfew capsules contain parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew capsules help to reduce the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to menstrual cramp pain.
Antispasmodic effects: Feverfew's antispasmodic properties help to relax smooth muscle tissue in the uterus, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstruation.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: A sesquiterpene lactone, parthenolide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Thujone: A terpene, thujone has a sedative effect and can help relax the uterine muscles, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of menstrual cramps.
Bornyl acetate: A terpene, bornyl acetate has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Tanacetum parthenium are the most commonly used part for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Flowers: The flowers are another key part used in Feverfew supplements due to their high concentration of parthenolide, a compound that helps to reduce menstrual cramp pain.
Stems: The stems of Tanacetum parthenium are also used, although less commonly, for their medicinal properties and to provide a consistent supply of parthenolide.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers at the peak of their potency.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250g of the powder with 250g of vegetable cellulose in a large bowl.
Add 50g of silica to the mixture and blend well for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain the essential oil of Lavandula angustifolia, which has a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect. When taken as a supplement, it can help to reduce the severity of menstrual cramps by relaxing muscles and improving blood flow. This calming agent also helps to balance hormones, reducing the production of prostaglandins that cause uterine contractions and ensuing pain. By promoting relaxation and soothing discomfort, English lavender capsules provide natural relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic compounds help to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lavender capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress related to menstrual cramp discomfort, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing the uterine muscles, thereby providing pain relief.
Linalyl acetate: Another terpene that contributes to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help in reducing the severity of menstrual cramps.
Lavandulol: A terpene that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing the uterine muscles and reducing menstrual pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are commonly used to make capsules for menstrual cramps due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties.
Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are another commonly used part for capsules, as they contain similar compounds to the flowers and are believed to help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.

Seeds: Lavandula angustifolia seeds are sometimes used to make capsules, although less commonly than flowers or leaves, due to their potential to provide a sustained release of bioactive compounds and a more subtle effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 lb of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of bloom and dry them for 7 days.
Grind 2 oz of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 part of the ground flowers with 1 part of gelatin in a bowl for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon for 30 minutes.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach heliotrope capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a potent blend of natural herbs that work together to ease the discomfort associated with PMS. Heliotrope, also known as beach heliotrope, has been traditionally used to soothe menstrual cramps and other symptoms of menstruation due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules also contain other herbs like ginger and turmeric, which help to reduce prostaglandins, a hormone responsible for causing uterine contractions that lead to cramping. This natural combination provides fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps, allowing women to feel more comfortable and relaxed during their period.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Beach heliotrope contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help ease menstrual cramp pain by decreasing prostaglandins' ability to constrict uterine muscles.
Antispasmodic properties: The plant's alkaloids, such as heliotropine, possess antispasmodic activity, which may help relax smooth muscle contractions in the uterus and alleviate menstrual cramps.

Pain-relieving glycosides: Beach heliotrope contains glycosides like helianthoside, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to reduce menstrual pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing menstrual cramp pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds with analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glehnia littoralis roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 250g of finely powdered cornstarch and 100g of glycerin in a bowl until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the glehnia littoralis mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years or refrigerate for up to 5 months.
Take one to two capsules orally three times daily as needed, starting 7 days before menstruation and continuing throughout the menstrual cycle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain flavonoids and essential oils that have a natural relaxant effect on the uterine muscles. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and ease spasms, thereby alleviating cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of hops may also help to reduce bloating and mood swings often experienced during this time, promoting a sense of relief and well-being throughout the menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hops contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce prostaglandins, the hormone-like substances responsible for menstrual cramp pain.
Saponins: Saponins in hops have a mild sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms associated with menstrual cramps, allowing for better pain management.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols present in hops have antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to relief from menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks are employed because they contain compounds that can help relax uterine muscles and ease menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and stems during the full moon for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 55 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind dried humulus lupulus into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of ground humulus lupulus with 2 tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil and let it infuse for 24 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or nut milk bag, then fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting liquid extract and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the uterus, reducing the severity of cramping. The bioactive constituents in yarrow, such as chamazulene and matricine, help to relax uterine muscle contractions, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with menstruation. Additionally, yarrow capsules can also help to regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to cramps, promoting a smoother and more manageable menstrual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterus and surrounding tissues, alleviating menstrual cramp discomfort.

Spasmolytic effects: The bioactive compounds in yarrow, such as sesquiterpenes and lignans, exhibit spasmolytic activity, meaning they can relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms that contribute to menstrual cramps.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow capsules contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect the body from oxidative stress and free radical damage, which can exacerbate menstrual cramp symptoms by causing inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, yarrow roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving menstrual cramp pain.
Leaves: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, the leaves of yarrow plant help to reduce prostaglandins, which contribute to menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves at dawn or just before sunset for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to enhance bioavailability.
Mix 250mg of the powdered achillea millefolium with 500mg of gelatin and 20ml of distilled water in a bowl to create a uniform mixture.
Fill small capsule shells with 1/4 teaspoon of the mixture and seal immediately, allowing for a minimum shelf life of 6 months when stored in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds help to relax uterine muscles, reduce spasms, and decrease prostaglandin production, which are all key contributors to menstrual cramp discomfort. By targeting the underlying causes of menstrual cramps, maidenhair tree capsules provide effective natural relief from symptoms, allowing women to manage their pain and discomfort more effectively during their periods.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maidenhair tree capsules contain flavonoids and saponins that relax smooth muscles, reducing uterine contractions and alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The capsules' antioxidants and phenolic compounds inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances responsible for causing inflammation and pain during menstruation.

Oxytocin-reducing properties: Adiantum capillus-veneris extracts have been shown to decrease oxytocin levels in the body, a hormone that can exacerbate menstrual cramps by stimulating uterine contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that help to relax uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity and duration.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair tree are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure 250 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 125 milliliters of hot water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Add 250 milliliters of glycerin to the liquid extract and mix well, allowing it to infuse for an additional 24 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the prepared ginkgo biloba extract and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain natural compounds that ease uterine contractions, reducing discomfort and pain. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, fenugreek has been shown to increase prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that help to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate cramps. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, fenugreek capsules provide natural relief from menstrual cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fenugreek capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like galactomannans and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
Pain-relieving properties: Fenugreek contains a compound called cuminaldehyde, which has been shown to have analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, helping to reduce menstrual cramps and discomfort.

Hormone-balancing effects: Fenugreek is believed to help balance hormonal levels in the body, particularly estrogen and prostaglandins, which can contribute to menstrual cramp severity. By regulating hormone levels, fenugreek capsules may help alleviate cramping symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots are a rich source of estrogenic compounds, making them effective in alleviating menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain a compound called galactomannan, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried fenugreek leaves and seeds in an amount of 100 grams for this recipe.
Grind the dried fenugreek into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground fenugreek powder with 200 grams of vegetable glycerin and 50 grams of distilled water to create a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-teaspoonful of the fenugreek paste, ensuring consistent dosing for each capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or until consumed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a natural compound called leonurine, which has been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce contractions. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leonurus cardiaca leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in shade or using low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours.
Grind dried leonurus cardiaca into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix one part powdered leonurus cardiaca with two parts carrier material such as cornstarch or potato starch for easy encapsulation.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared blend and seal securely, storing in a cool dark place until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken regularly, herbal licorice capsules can help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances in the uterus that cause muscle contractions and pain during menstruation. This natural remedy can provide relief from discomfort, stiffness, and cramping associated with PMS, allowing women to manage their symptoms more effectively and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the uterus, thereby alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Isoflavonoids: These compounds have a synergistic effect with glycyrrhizin, enhancing its anti-inflammatory properties and also exhibiting their own analgesic and anti-spasmotic actions, further contributing to relief from menstrual cramps.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have antioxidant and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menstruation, leading to reduced pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate menstrual cramp pain and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps relax smooth muscle tissue and reduces uterine contractions, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them for at least seven days under shade.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root with 250 milliliters of glycerin and 50 milliliters of water to form a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the herbal paste, then seal them immediately using a capsule machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before using as needed during menstrual periods.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin. Salicin works by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping. By reducing prostaglandins, white willow capsules help alleviate cramp severity and duration, providing relief from menstrual discomfort. Additionally, salicin has anti-inflammatory properties, which may also contribute to its pain-relieving effects on menstrual cramps. This natural alternative offers a safe and effective way to manage menstrual cramps without resorting to over-the-counter pain medications.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve menstrual cramp discomfort.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in white willow have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions leading to menstrual cramps.
Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate, a compound derived from salicin, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to alleviate menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.

Roots: White willow roots contain a higher concentration of salicin than other parts of the plant, making them an effective natural remedy for reducing menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba leaves and dry them completely using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 2 days.
Grind dried salix alba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground salix alba with 250mg of organic silica and 125mg of vitamin e oil to create the herbal blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend, ensuring each capsule contains exactly 875mg.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 12 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain apigenin, a natural compound that has been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce inflammation. Myristicin: Myristicin, another compound present in parsley, has analgesic and antispasmodic effects that can help soothe menstrual cramp pain and relax the uterus, reducing spasms and discomfort.

Folate: Parsley is rich in folate, a B vitamin that helps regulate hormonal balance and reduces homocysteine levels, which may contribute to menstrual cramps by promoting inflammation and vascular constriction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh petroselinum crispum leaves at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind 5 grams of dried petroselinum crispum into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground herb with 250 milliliters of cold water and let it steep for 20 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the prepared herbal extract and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and relax the uterine lining. remedy_recipe: Measure 100g of dried taraxacum officinale root into a glass container.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered root with 30g of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 50ml of hot distilled water to the bowl and stir until the mixture is smooth for 2 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture and store in an airtight container.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to menstrual cramps that are treated with herbal capsules are headaches, migraines, and joint pain. Capsules containing herbs like Willow bark, Feverfew, and Ginger help alleviate these conditions by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving blood circulation. Additionally, herbs like Valerian root and Passionflower can help ease muscle tension and promote relaxation, providing relief from cramp-like symptoms in the body.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with menstrual cramps is a blend of cramp bark, ginger, and chasteberry. Cramp bark helps to relax the uterine muscles, while ginger reduces inflammation and soothes the stomach. Chasteberry, also known as vitex, has a balancing effect on hormonal imbalances that can contribute to menstrual cramps. This combination works together to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, promoting a sense of calm and relief during the menstrual cycle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a blend of natural compounds that work to ease uterine contractions and relax the muscles. The herb, also known as black cohosh, has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of PMS, including cramping, bloating, and mood swings. Isoferulagones: Isoferulagones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation, as well as promote overall well-being during menstrual cramps.

remedy_recipe: Dry 2 cups of cimicifuga racemosa root in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 1 cup of dried cimicifuga racemosa root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground cimicifuga racemosa powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water.
Pour the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and store them in an airtight container.
Label the capsules with the date and contents and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

Roots: Additionally, the roots have a soothing effect on the nervous system, which helps to alleviate anxiety and stress associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora root in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Dry the harvested root in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 200 milligrams of the ground root powder with 100 milligrams of gelatin and 20 milligrams of glycerin.

Crush the dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powdered angelica sinensis with 0.5g of a binding agent like gum arabic in a small bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the angelica sinensis mixture using a capsule filler for 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they work by regulating hormonal imbalances that can lead to painful periods. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers at peak potency typically between june and september.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250g of the powder with 250g of tapioca starch and 50g of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powder mixture each using a capsule filler.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with menstrual cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves in late autumn when the trees are fully dormant for 3-4 weeks.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500 mg of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50 mg of silica gel as a capsule filler for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for 10 minutes.

Dry the flowers in a single layer at a temperature of 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain high levels of isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. Crush the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 125 milliliters of vegetable glycerin with 250 milliliters of distilled water and 10 grams of the powdered material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with menstrual cramps because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces prostaglandin production. Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 grams of the ground curcuma longa powder with 125 grams of gelatin and 125 grams of water in a bowl.
